title: hot bites april new restaurants menus
full_text: Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: April’s Hot Bites: 7 New Spots And Menus That Deserve A Return Visit 1 01 April’s Hot Bites: 7 New Spots And Menus That Deserve A Return Visit Main// Eat// Drink// Reviews// Insider// At Home// About Us// Download Our Magazine// Q2 2026// Q1 2026// Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026// Home Eat April’s Hot Bites: 7 New Spots And Menus That Deserve A Return VisitApril’s Hot Bites: 7 New Spots And Menus That Deserve A Return VisitPatrick LohEat2 months agoShare These seven new openings and menus across Klang Valley are tested and cleared for repeat visits. From short-run pop-ups to new permanent fixtures, here are the ones earning a second booking. Welcome to the first edition of Hot Bites, our monthly sweep of what’s new, what’s worth it, and what deserves your appetite in Klang Valley. No filler, just seven openings and menus we’ve actually sat through, all tried and tested. If you’re wondering where to go, start here. Stereotype, Section 17 Part café, part listening room, Stereotype draws in audiophiles and night owls alike. The space leans bold and design-forward, tuned as carefully as the playlists. On the menu: cappuccino pasta, sambal garlic butter tiger prawn spaghetti served in a mug, and a line-up of zero-proof coolers that hold their own. Address: 617A, 1st Floor, Jalan 17/10, Seksyen 17, 46400 Petaling Jaya, Selangor.Booking: +6012 213 1350IG: @stereotype.pj Photo courtesy of Stereotype Bobby’s Burger, The Exchange TRX You’ve seen him all over the internet: Ben Sumadiwiria—better known as Bobby Saputra—now has a physical address called Bobby’s Burgers at The Exchange TRX. Go for the Angus beef patties finished with his house Billionaire Sauce. The queue is incredibly long for this one, so head over before the dinner crowd arrives. Address: The Exchange TRX mall, Level Concourse.IG: @officialbobbysburgers Photo: Manu Ros | Unsplash 90D, Conlay by E&O Something rather fabulous is bubbling away in KL: the 90-day pop-up by Chef Toraik Chua pulls from a three-Michelin-starred pedigree, moving through leek broth, giblets, king crab with caviar, grouper, and wagyu, with sake and wine pacing the night. Then Jun Ong takes over, lighting the room into something closer to theatre than dining. It runs from 1 April for exactly 90 days, then it disappears. No encore promised. Address: Conlay by E&O. 11, Jalan Conlay, 50450, Kuala Lumpur.Booking: thisis90d.com/bookingWebsite: thisis90d.comIG: @thisis90d Photo courtesy of This Is 90D Torino Toriko, Lot 10 Straight out of Tokyo, Torino Toriko lands as a pop-up with skewers over flame and a deeply savoury chicken ramen. Compact, fast-moving, and built for sitting close—like a backstreet stop in Shinjuku—this one is worth the trip. Address: Lot 10 Shopping Centre, Level 4. 50, Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur.IG: @torinotoriko_kl Photo: Kelsey He | Unsplash Sabato’s, Kimpton Naluria KL At Sabato’s, American-Italian comfort gets a confident polish—lasagna stacked high, meatballs that don’t skimp, and arancini with a crisp shell. Head over to the newly launched Kimpton Naluria for this savoury feast. Address: Level 25, Kimpton Naluria, Lingkaran TRX, Tun Razak Exchange, 55188 Kuala Lumpur.Booking: +6019 850 1785Website: kimptonhotels.comIG: @sabatoskl Photo courtesy of Sabato’s Apartment 80, REXKL For dessert, head up to Apartment 80 at REXKL—a rooftop spot that trades the noise for a little breathing room. On the menu: Frozen Chocolate Mousse, Tea-ramisu, and a line-up of coffee, matcha, and non-caffeinated drinks that keep things going without tipping the balance. Address: 3rd Floor, REXKL. 80, Jalan Sultan 80, 50000, Kuala Lumpur.IG: @apartment80.kl Photo courtesy of Apartment 80 New Menu Alert Zipangu, Shangri-La KL At Zipangu, Chef Kazuo Takizawa is running a tight, sea bream–focused programme. The Ainan Red Sea Bream menu spans four, five, or seven courses, moving from lightly seared cuts to sashimi, then into warmer territory like sencha broth, a crisp katsu sando, Japanese-style acqua pazza and a salt-baked fish brushed with soy. The fish leaves on 22 May, so catch it while you can. Address: 11, Jalan Sultan Ismail, 50250 Kuala Lumpur.Booking: Shangri-la.com/bookingWebsite: shangri-la.comIG: @shangrilakl Photo courtesy of Shangri-La KL Opening photo: Courtesy of This Is 90D Hot BitesNew MenusNew RestaurantsShare Leave a reply Cancel reply Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.
